meta.title: Sensor-fault drill
meta.subtitle: pH sensor sticks then drops out; the safety layer holds dosing
meta.provenance_page: All series simulated; see per-series provenance.

series_provenance: synthetic scenario generator (seeded) → TGF physics engine

indices.provenance: tgf_dosing physics engine (LSI, RSI via vendored cooling-tower-chem formulas); PSI from the Puckorius equilibrium pH

anomalies_provenance: tgf_dosing statistical anomaly detector (z-score + rate-of-change + persistence; MOMENT not used)

rationale: pH probe reading is outside the plausible range and has flatlined — the reading cannot be trusted.
Sensor-sanity interlock failed, so the safety layer holds all dosing until the probe is verified.
Indices derived from the faulted probe are suppressed; no chemical is delivered on bad data.
recommendation.projected: dosing held — no chemical delivered while the pH probe is faulted (simulated)

safety_checks.name: Sensor sanity
safety_checks.detail: pH probe outside plausible range [3.0–12.0] for ≥3 cycles — dosing held

safety_checks.name: Dose rate limit
safety_checks.detail: ≤ 30% step change and ≤ 10 kg/hr pump limit

safety_checks.name: Daily cumulative cap
safety_checks.detail: 17.49 kg dosed in last 24 h vs 240 kg cap

safety_checks.name: Discharge interlock (CoC / CPCB pH)
safety_checks.detail: CoC 3.3 within 8 limit; discharge pH check deferred while the probe is faulted

recommendation.provenance: tgf_dosing MPC optimizer + safety layer, run offline; trajectories from the TGF simulator. Closed-loop is backtest-only.

alerts.text: Anomaly on ph — pH=0.00, z-score=-8.65 (mean=7.42, σ=0.86)
pH sensor sanity check failed — dosing held

lab.provenance: synthetic lab panel (seeded), sampled every 12 h — presented as manually entered lab results

timeline.provenance: TGF MPC optimizer + safety layer evaluated hourly in the offline run

axes.label: Scaling margin
axes.formula: 100 · clip((1.0 − LSI) / 1.5, 0, 1) — distance of LSI below the +1.0 scaling threshold

axes.label: Corrosion margin
axes.formula: 100 · min(clip((LSI + 0.5) / 1.0, 0, 1), clip((7.5 − RSI) / 1.5, 0, 1)) — LSI above −0.5 and RSI below 7.5

axes.label: Inhibitor adequacy
axes.formula: 100 · clip((residual − band lo) / (target − band lo), 0, 1) against the chemical program band

axes.label: Data health
axes.formula: 100 − 60·(sensor fault active) − 12·(anomaly episodes in prior 24 h), floored at 0

axes.label: Safety headroom
axes.formula: 0 if dosing held, else 100 · (1 − inhibitor dosed in prior 24 h / daily cap)

axes.label: Forecast margin
axes.formula: 100 · clip((band hi − conductivity q90 at +24 h) / (0.15 · band hi), 0, 1); 50 when the forecast is suppressed

radar.provenance: axis scores computed from the simulated run by generate_data.py; formulas shown per axis

correlation.note: Pearson correlation over the simulated hourly series; the fault drill's pH column includes the stuck/dropout readings on purpose.

inventory.basis: average simulated use over the window

ops.provenance: operations layer simulated for the demo — pump, inventory and log entries derive from the dosing timeline and a seeded schedule
